title: The High-Canopy Archive Station

features.name: archive node hardware
features.desc-on-touch: The burl casing, antenna coiled behind loose bark. 300 bits per second rising into the canopy. Batteries good for fifty years.

features.name: leaf-record stack
features.desc-on-touch: Files translated into the language of leaves—photosynthesis rates, water-memory, light-angles at noon. The tree reads itself and teaches the archive to listen.

features.name: signal cascade
features.desc-on-touch: Where the tree's crown touches the transmission, and 300 bits become indistinguishable from wind in the high branches.

features.name: young-leaf silence
features.desc-on-touch: The moment before new growth knows it is being recorded. You stand in the pause between photosynthesis and archive.

features.name: crown threshold
features.desc-on-touch: The point where the tree stops growing upward and starts keeping records downward into its rings.

inscription: here the tree remembers it is alive by filing what it has become

file.name: Canopy_Node_Installation_Log_2012-Present.txt
file.note: Hardware record: rings counting down, batteries aging, the tree's own growth consuming the casing year by year, archive and timber learning to share one space

exits.label: down toward younger leaves that haven't learned to read
higher, where the antenna breaks through into weather
along the branch-network toward another station's heartbeat
